questions.q:
If the equation
has four distinct solutions then the number of integral values of $k$ is
questions.solution:
$\lim _{t \rightarrow \infty} \frac{\left(t^2-2\right)}{t^2\left(\sqrt{2-\frac{1}{t}-\frac{1}{t^2}}+\sqrt{1-\frac{1}{t}+\frac{1}{t^2}}\right)(\sqrt{2}-1)}=1$
Now || $\mathrm{x}-1|-6|=\mathrm{k}$ has four distinct solutions if $\mathrm{k} \in(0,6)$
$\therefore$ Number of integral values of k is 5
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{(1-\cos 2 x)(3+\cos x)}{x \tan 4 x}$ is equal to:
questions.solution:
$\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{(1-\cos 2 x)(3+\cos x)}{x \tan 4 x}$

Divide by $x^2$ in Numerator and denominator
$\begin{aligned} & \lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{2\left(\frac{\sin ^2 x}{x^2}\right)(3+\cos x)}{\frac{x \tan 4 x}{x^2}} \\ & \lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{2\left(\frac{\sin x}{x}\right)^2(3+\cos x)}{4\left(\frac{\tan 4 x}{4 x}\right)} \\ & =\frac{2 \times 1^2 \times(3+1)}{4 \times 1}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Consider, $f(x)=\left\{\begin{array}{ll}\frac{e^{(x-2) \ln \sqrt{5^x}+\frac{1}{3 x} \log _2\left(\left(4^x\right)^3\right)}-e^2}{(x-2) \tan x}, & x\gt0 \\ \lambda \frac{\left((\sqrt{5})^x-1\right)\left(e^{x^2+2}-e^2\right)}{(2 \sin x-\sin 2 x)}, & x \lt 0\end{array}\right.$. If $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} f(x)$ exists then $\lambda$ is equal to
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \lim _{x \rightarrow 0^{+}} e^2 \frac{\left(5^{\left(\frac{x(x-2)}{2}\right)}-1\right)}{\frac{2 x(x-2)}{2} \frac{\tan x}{x}}=\frac{1}{2} e^2 \ln 5 \\ & \lim _{x \rightarrow 0^{-}} \frac{\lambda\left(e^2\left((\sqrt{5})^x-1\right)\right)\left(e^{x^2}-1\right)}{x \frac{2 \sin x}{x}\left(\frac{1-\cos x}{x^2}\right) x^2}=\lambda e^2 \ln \sqrt{5}=\lambda \frac{e^2}{2} \ln 5 \\ & \therefore \lambda=1\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: If $\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{n \cdot 3^n}{n(x-2)^2+n \cdot 3^{n+1}-3^n}=\frac{1}{3}$ then the range of $x$ is $(n \in N)$
questions.solution:
$\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{1}{\frac{(x-2)^n}{3^n}+3-\frac{1}{n}}$ divided $N$ and $D$ by $3^n \cdot n$
$\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty}$ to equal to $\frac{1}{3} \Rightarrow \lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{1}{n} \rightarrow 0$
$\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty}\left(\frac{x-2}{3}\right)^n \rightarrow 0 \Rightarrow-1 \lt x \lt 5$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f:(1, \infty) \rightarrow(0, \infty)$ be a continuous decreasing function with $\lim _{x \rightarrow \infty} \frac{f(4 x)}{f(8 x)}=1$, then $\lim _{x \rightarrow \infty} \frac{f(6 x)}{f(8 x)}$ is equal to
questions.solution:
As $f(x)$ is continuous decreasing function on $(0, \infty)$, so $0 \lt f(8 x) \lt f(6 x) \lt f(4 x)$
So $\lim _{x \rightarrow \infty} 1 \lt \lim _{x \rightarrow \infty} \frac{f(6 x)}{f(8 x)} \lt \lim _{x \rightarrow \infty} \frac{f(4 x)}{f(8 x)}$
By squeeze play theorem $\lim _{x \rightarrow \infty} \frac{f(6 x)}{f(8 x)}=1$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f:[0,1] \rightarrow R$ be a differentiable function with $f(0)=0$, then $\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} n^2 \int_0^{1 / n} f(t) d t$ equals
questions.solution:
$I=\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{\int_0^{1 / n} f(t) d t}{\frac{1}{n^2}}\left(\frac{0}{0} \text { form }\right)$
Using : L' Hospital and using Leibnitz rule
$I=\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{n^3 f\left(\frac{1}{n}\right)\left(-\frac{1}{n^2}\right)}{-2}=\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{n}{2} f\left(\frac{1}{n}\right)$
Put $\mathrm{n}=\frac{1}{\mathrm{~h}}$ hence
$I=\frac{1}{2} \times \lim _{h \rightarrow 0}\left(\frac{f(0+h)-f(0)}{h}\right)=\frac{1}{2} f^{\prime}(0)$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
